title: Microsoft confirmed to backup BitLocker keys in plain-text, hands them over to law enforcement
description: Going against standard practice in the industry, Microsoft backs-up your BitLocker Recovery Keys in plain-text, rather than in an end-to-end encrypted form. Apple, on the other hand, backs-up your FileVault Recovery Key end-to-end encrypted as part of your keychain, rendering it entirely unreadable by them and, by extension, law enforcement.

sources.title: Microsoft Gave FBI Keys To Unlock Encrypted Data, Exposing Major Privacy Flaw

title: Canada Computers' website compromised, card-skimming malware injected
description: The website of Canadian electronics retailer Canada Computers was compromised by an attacker who injected card-skimming malware on payment pages. Initially publicized on Reddit by a customer at least 18 days after initially injected, Canada Computers finally took action the same day and began notifying impacted customers 4 days later.

sources.title: Canada Computers online card skimmer
Canada Computers customers say retailer’s website compromised their credit cards
Major Canada Computers Breach: Customers Warned to Cancel Cards
Data breach at Canada Computers & Electronics leaks personal customer information

title: President Trump's acting head of CISA uploads documents to ChatGPT
description: The interim director of the US Cybersecurity and Infrastructure Security Agency, Madhu Gottumukkala, uploaded government documents to a public version of ChatGPT. These documents were marked “for official use only”, and not authorized for public disclosure. CISA flagged the uploads in August of 2025, and conducted an internal review to assess potential impacts. Officials claim that despite the availability of DHS's DHSChat, Gottumukkala strongarmed CISA into giving him access to ChatGPT.

sources.title: Trump’s acting cyber chief uploaded sensitive files into a public version of ChatGPT

title: Dutch authorities seize VPN server without warrant
description: Windscribe, a Canadian VPN provider, had one of its servers seized by Dutch authorities, with the promise to return it once they "fully analyze it." Windscribe has a no-logs policy and claims the server taken was using RAM disks, so no private user data should be found.

sources.title: X post from Windscribe about the incident
Dutch authorities allegedly seize VPN server without a warrant — company claims that law enforcement will return it after analyzing the device fully

furtherReading.title: My Big Fat Greek Case (A court case in Greece proving the no-logs policy)

title: Discord announces upcoming global rollout of age verification
description: Discord adds age verification, where certain spaces and features are now restricted unless a user is verified to be an adult. Age verification will be implemented via a combination of automated age estimation, "video selfies", and uploaded identity documents. Discord claims that all video selfie processing is performed locally and that the video never leaves the user's device. For users that upload identity documents, they claim these documents will be deleted "quickly".

sources.title: Discord Launches Teen Default Experience Globally

furtherReading.title: Discord Voluntarily Pushes Mandatory Age Verification Despite Recent Data Breach
'I do not trust them' - top streamers left concerned by Discord age checks

title: Critical Notepad vulnerability linked to new Markdown feature
description: The Windows 11 Notepad app, recently upgraded with AI features, now carries a high-severity flaw that exposes users to dangerous attacks. Hackers can trick Windows users into clicking a malicious link inside a Markdown file, opened by default by the Notepad app on most Windows systems. This would then cause Notepad to launch unverified protocols that load and execute remote files/code with the same permissions as the user. The vulnerability, assigned CVE-2026-20841, has a severity rating of 8.8 out of 10. While not directly caused by AI, this vulnerability highlights the risks of rapid feature expansion in established software.

sources.title: Critical Notepad vulnerability reignites criticism of Microsoft’s forced AI features

title: Discord revealed to be collaborating with Persona
description: Discord reveals that they lied about age-verification processing information never leaving a user's device and are actually using a server-side age-verification service-provider named Persona for a subset of users in the UK. They quickly retracted the warning shortly after releasing it on their website.

sources.title: Fury over Discord’s age checks explodes after shady Persona test in UK

title: Discord cuts ties with Persona
description: Persona is found to have front-end code accessible on the internet and on government servers. Around 2500 files were found to have been accessible for the governments, and apparently, Persona performed facial recognition checks against watchlists and lists of people of power. Discord has since announced that it is cutting ties with Persona.

sources.title: Discord cuts ties with Peter Thiel–backed verification software after its code was found tied to U.S. surveillance efforts

furtherReading.title: Getting Global Age Assurance Right: What We Got Wrong and What's Changing

title: Security focused mobile OS Graphene announces new hardware partnership
description: GrapheneOS, a privacy and security focused mobile OS based on Android, revealed a new hardware partnership with Motorola. Current versions of the OS only work on Google phones, as those are the only devices to meet their hardware security standards. This partnership promises compatibility with new, security focused Motorola phone models allowing more hardware options for security conscious users.

sources.title: Motorola partnership announcement
Motorola announces a partnership with GrapheneOS Foundation...

furtherReading.title: GrapheneOS: the private and secure mobile OS

title: Instagram ending support for end-to-end encrypted direct messages
description: Meta has updated Instagram's help pages, stating that E2EE DMs will no longer be supported as of May 8th, 2026. E2EE ensures that messages cannot be meaningfully intercepted by third parties, including Meta, serving as a key mechanism of user privacy. Meta also maintains Messenger and WhatsApp, potentially foreshadowing incoming changes to these platforms as well.

sources.title: X post from stupidtechtakes providing initial report of the upcoming change
Meta To Remove End-to-End Encryption on Instagram DMs From May 8
What is end-to-end encryption on Instagram
Meta is killing end-to-end encryption for Instagram DMs soon
Instagram quietly drops end-to-end encrypted chats

title: Lloyds banking app glitch exposes customer transaction data
description: A glitch in Lloyds Banking Group’s mobile banking apps allowed some customers to see other users' transaction data and personal information. The incident affected Lloyds, Halifax, and Bank of Scotland customers and is an example of a privacy breach caused by a software failure rather than a hack.

sources.title: Lloyds bank reveals IT glitch affected almost half a million customers
Nearly half a million Lloyds customers hit by data breach after IT glitch
